one_liner: OKLO at $49.27 bounced +4.21% from Monday's $47.90 low — RSI 38.8 remains near oversold and price is below Wyckoff support ($49.45); the Groves criticality test narrative supports watching for a reversal.

price_action.intraday_story: OKLO bounced from a low of $47.29 (below Monday's low of $46.95) to close at $49.27. The intraday low briefly undercut Monday's support but recovered — a potential Spring pattern. Volume at 0.59x average confirms this is not yet a high-conviction reversal.

news_catalyst.headline: Why OKLO Is Betting Big on the Future of Nuclear Isotopes — July Criticality Test in View
news_catalyst.summary: OKLO's DOE safety approval for the Groves Isotope Test Reactor (July 1) remains the key catalyst. The article highlights OKLO's isotope production strategy as a differentiated revenue stream beyond power generation. The July criticality test timeline is the binary event investors are watching — any confirmation of this milestone would be a significant positive catalyst.

technical.summary: OKLO briefly undercut Monday's $46.95 low to $47.29 before recovering — a textbook Spring setup. RSI at 38.8 is near oversold. EMA50 ($53.71) and EMA200 ($54.02) are overhead resistance. Wyckoff support is at $49.45 — the stock is just below this level at $49.27. A close above $49.45 on expanding volume would confirm the Spring and target the $53-54 EMA cluster.
technical.wyckoff_narrative: The intraday undercut of the $47.90 Monday low to $47.29 followed by recovery is a classic Wyckoff Spring. The Spring hypothesis requires confirmation: a subsequent session with a volume-expanding close above the Wyckoff support at $49.45 would confirm absorption and set up a move toward $53-54 (EMAs). Until that confirmation arrives, this is a watch rather than accumulate.

valuation.summary: Pre-revenue nuclear reactor company — DCF not applicable. Wall Street consensus: 19 analysts, mean target $88.63 (80%+ upside). Next earnings August 10.

key_strengths: DOE Reactor Pilot Program approval secured July 1
key_concerns: Zero revenue — all value is optionality on reactor deployment timeline
C grade (0.557) reflects pre-revenue speculative stage
Criticality test delay risk is the key binary event
fundamentals.summary: C grade with strong analyst coverage but no revenue. Watch for the Groves criticality test milestone. Earnings August 10.

risks: Spring confirmation unverified — needs close above $49.45 on volume >1.3x average
Criticality test delay would remove the primary near-term catalyst
Earnings August 10 — continued cash burn disclosure without revenue milestone would pressure stock
